sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Everton have struggled recently, winless in their last 3 matches with only 2 points from 5 games, showing defensive vulnerabilities by conceding in all 7 recent matches. Fulham, on the other hand, have shown mixed form, with a strong 3-0 win over Wolverhampton but only 1 win in their last 5 matches, indicating inconsistency away from home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Everton have had the edge, winning 3-1 at Fulham and drawing 1-1 at home. Historical data suggests tight games, often with under 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 meetings, pointing to a potentially low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Everton rank 14th with 12 points, slightly ahead of Fulham at 15th with 11 points, both showing mid-table struggles. Everton's defense has conceded 13 goals, while Fulham have scored 12 but also conceded 14, highlighting both teams’ issues with consistency in both attack and defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Everton will miss key midfielder Iliman Ndiaye, which could impact their creativity, but players like James Tarkowski remain crucial in defense. Fulham have no major absences and rely on Alex Iwobi’s playmaking, with goalkeeper Bernd Leno’s reliability in goal being a standout factor.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Everton’s home advantage and Sofascore’s prediction of a 40% chance of a home win, they are slight favorites. However, Fulham’s ability to score first in recent head-to-heads and Everton’s defensive woes suggest a close contest, likely ending in a draw or narrow Everton victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Hill Dickinson Stadium saw Everton take a narrow 1-0 lead over Fulham with a late goal from Idrissa Gueye just before the break. Everton dominated much of the possession and created more dangerous opportunities, evidenced by their higher number of final third entries and penalty area penetrations. Fulham, on the other hand, struggled to impose themselves, failing to create significant chances while also picking up more disciplinary cautions with two yellow cards compared to Everton's one. The home side's aerial dominance and overall duel-winning ability further underlined their control of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: Everton started the game with strong attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages with frequent attacking spells. There was a noticeable shift in momentum around the midway point of the half, as Fulham managed a brief period of sustained pressure, pushing forward with intent. However, Everton regained their attacking rhythm soon after, particularly in the latter stages of the half, culminating in their goal just before halftime. Fulham had moments of threat but lacked the consistency to truly challenge Everton's defense during these attacking phases.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Everton, Idrissa Gueye stood out with his crucial goal, proving to be a pivotal figure in midfield. Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall also impressed with his defensive contributions and ability to win tackles, earning a high rating for his efforts. Iliman Ndiaye contributed creatively, setting up a big chance for his teammates. On the Fulham side, Bernd Leno was a standout in goal, keeping his team in the game despite the pressure. Calvin Bassey showed resilience in defense, while Alex Iwobi and Raúl Jiménez offered some threat going forward, though without the end product.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Everton displayed a well-organized approach in the first half, focusing on maintaining possession and exploiting the final third with effective entries and penalty area presence. Their ability to win aerial duels and overall physical battles allowed them to dictate the tempo. Fulham, however, appeared disjointed at times, with limited success in the final third and a lack of cohesion in their attacking play. Their defensive structure held up under pressure, but the lack of creativity and clinical finishing restricted their ability to respond to Everton's dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Everton are likely to continue their assertive approach, leveraging their momentum from the late first-half goal to push for more scoring opportunities. Fulham will need to adapt quickly, possibly introducing fresh legs to inject energy and creativity into their attack. The away side must improve their final third presence and capitalize on any defensive lapses from Everton to get back into the game. Discipline will also be crucial for Fulham, as avoiding further bookings could help them maintain their shape and focus.
